page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory footprints relative to compute resources. It features 40 vCPUs mapped to 20 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 961.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 24.02 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core compute and multi-core memory capacity. Single-core performance is positioned in the bottom 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 1520.54 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 29.28 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server delivers high memory density, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound applications rather than compute-intensive processing. The lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, which should be factored into the overall infrastructure architecture.

This instance is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-intensive data processing workloads like Redis operations, where it achieves an average of 21.2 million operations per second. It is also suitable for medium-scale language model inference, though it exhibits poor performance on larger 70B models.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 40 dedicated vCPUs and 961.0 GB of RAM, yielding 24.02 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz across 20 physical cores, this instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data reveals a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution is weak, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. Qualitatively, this server offers high memory density for memory-bound workloads, though compute-heavy tasks may find the 2.2 GHz clock speed limiting. It is highly suited for large in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 40 vCPUs, 961 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, designed for large in-memory databases and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-40 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 40 vCPUs and 961 GB RAM, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity for memory-bound enterprise applications and large in-memory databases.
bullet_points: 40 dedicated vCPUs and 961 GB of RAM providing 24.02 GB per core
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 20 physical cores running at 2.2 GHz
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Optimized for large in-memory databases, Redis, and enterprise ERP systems
